code:
import io
import string

fn hashes_for(_ c: Int) -> String
  mut h = ""
  mut k = 0
  while k < c
    h = "#{h}#"
    k = k + 1
  end
  h
end

let line = match io.read_line()
when Some(s)
  s
when None
  ""
end

mut c0 = 0
mut c1 = 0
mut c2 = 0
mut c3 = 0
mut c4 = 0
mut c5 = 0
mut c6 = 0
mut c7 = 0
mut c8 = 0
mut c9 = 0

let n = string.len(line)
mut i = 0
while i < n
  match string.byte_at(line, i)
  when Some(b)
    match b
    when 48
      c0 = c0 + 1
    when 49
      c1 = c1 + 1
    when 50
      c2 = c2 + 1
    when 51
      c3 = c3 + 1
    when 52
      c4 = c4 + 1
    when 53
      c5 = c5 + 1
    when 54
      c6 = c6 + 1
    when 55
      c7 = c7 + 1
    when 56
      c8 = c8 + 1
    when 57
      c9 = c9 + 1
    when _
      ()
    end
  when None
    ()
  end
  i = i + 1
end

println("0: #{hashes_for(c0)}")
println("1: #{hashes_for(c1)}")
println("2: #{hashes_for(c2)}")
println("3: #{hashes_for(c3)}")
println("4: #{hashes_for(c4)}")
println("5: #{hashes_for(c5)}")
println("6: #{hashes_for(c6)}")
println("7: #{hashes_for(c7)}")
println("8: #{hashes_for(c8)}")
println("9: #{hashes_for(c9)}")
